Data reading and clone correction: only populations with at least six samples are selected

dat1 <- read.vcfR("md6ct95ms200.vcf")
## Scanning file to determine attributes.
## File attributes:
##   meta lines: 10
##   header_line: 11
##   variant count: 25099
##   column count: 326
## Meta line 10 read in.
## All meta lines processed.
## gt matrix initialized.
## Character matrix gt created.
##   Character matrix gt rows: 25099
##   Character matrix gt cols: 326
##   skip: 0
##   nrows: 25099
##   row_num: 0
## Processed variant 1000Processed variant 2000Processed variant 3000Processed variant 4000Processed variant 5000Processed variant 6000Processed variant 7000Processed variant 8000Processed variant 9000Processed variant 10000Processed variant 11000Processed variant 12000Processed variant 13000Processed variant 14000Processed variant 15000Processed variant 16000Processed variant 17000Processed variant 18000Processed variant 19000Processed variant 20000Processed variant 21000Processed variant 22000Processed variant 23000Processed variant 24000Processed variant 25000Processed variant: 25099
## All variants processed
clones <- c(
  "DE1-2","DE1-4","DE1-7","DE1-5",
  "DE2-2","DE2-1","DE2-3","DE2-4",
  "DE3-9","DE3-10","DE3-11","DE3-5",
  "DE4-1","DE4-3","DE4-6","DE4-8",
  "DE5-1","DE5-10","DE5-3","DE5-2","DE5-4","DE5-6","DE5-7","DE5-9",
  "DE6-4","DE6-5","DE6-6","DE6-7",
  "DE7-12","DE7-6","DE7-7","DE7-10","DE7-2","DE7-17","DE7-18","DE7-19","DE7-20",
  "DE9-13",
  "DE10-1","DE10-10","DE10-11","DE10-12","DE10-2","DE10-3","DE10-5","DE10-7","DE10-8",
  "DE11-12","DE11-15","DE12-5",
  "DK-4","DK-5","DK-8","DK-9",
  "EE-5","EE-6","EE-7",
  "FI1-7-1","FI5-2","FI5-10","FI5-8",
  "LT1-9","LT2-1","LT2-2","LT2-3","LT2-4","LT2-5","LT2-6","LT2-7",
  "LT3-10","LT3-11","LT3-12","LT3-14","LT3-5","LT3-7","LT3-9",
  "LV-10","LV-12","LV-8","LV-9",
  "PL2-4","PL2-6-2",
  "PL4-14","PL4-1","PL4-3","PL4-7",
  "PL5-15","PL5-19","PL5-20","PL5-21","PL5-4",
  "PL6-10","PL6-9",
  "PL7-12","PL7-5","PL7-18",
  "PL8-17","PL8-8","PL9-4","PL9-8",
  "RU1-4","RU2-1","RU2-2","RU2-3","RU2-4","RU2-5","RU2-6",
  "SE3-4-2","SE1-1","SE1-10","SE1-4","SE1-5","SE1-6","SE1-7","SE1-9",
  "UA1-13","UA1-14","UA1-15","UA1-17",
  "UA2-1","UA2-12","UA2-18","UA2-3")

length(clones)
## [1] 123
table(colnames(dat1@gt)[-1] %in% clones)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
##   194   123
# Remove clones:
keep <- !colnames(dat1@gt)[-1] %in% clones
dat2 <- dat1
dat2@gt <- dat1@gt[, c(TRUE, keep)]
dat2.pre_gl <- vcfR2genlight(dat2)
## Warning in vcfR2genlight(dat2): Found 245 loci with more than two alleles.
## Objects of class genlight only support loci with two alleles.
## 245 loci will be omitted from the genlight object.
ploidy(dat2.pre_gl) <- 2
dat2.pre_gl <- gl.compliance.check(dat2.pre_gl)
## Starting gl.compliance.check 
##   Processing genlight object with SNP data
##   Warning: data include loci that are scored NA across all individuals.
##   Consider filtering using gl <- gl.filter.allna(gl)
##   The slot loc.all, which stores allele name for each locus, is empty. 
## Creating a dummy variable (A/C) to insert in this slot. 
##   Checking coding of SNPs
##     SNP data scored NA, 0, 1 or 2 confirmed
##   Checking locus metrics and flags
##   Recalculating locus metrics
##   Checking for monomorphic loci
##     Dataset contains monomorphic loci
##   Checking for loci with all missing data
##     Dataset contains loci with all missing dat
##   Checking whether individual names are unique.
##   Checking for individual metrics
##   Warning: Creating a slot for individual metrics
##   Checking for population assignments
##   Population assignments not detected, individuals assigned
##                     to a single population labelled 'pop1'
##   Spelling of coordinates checked and changed if necessary to 
##             lat/lon
## Completed: gl.compliance.check
dat2.pre_gl <- gl.filter.allna(dat2.pre_gl)
## Starting gl.filter.allna 
##   Processing genlight object with SNP data
##   Identifying and removing loci and individuals scored all 
##                 missing (NA)
##   Deleting loci that are scored as all missing (NA)
##   Deleted
##   Deleting individuals that are scored as all missing (NA)
## Completed: gl.filter.allna
dat2.pre_gl$ind.names
##   [1] "DE11-1"   "DE11-10"  "DE11-11"  "DE11-14"  "DE11-14r" "DE11-16" 
##   [7] "DE11-17"  "DE11-2"   "DE11-3"   "DE11-4"   "DE11-5"   "DE11-6"

## [181] "UA1-1"    "UA1-10"   "UA1-12"   "UA1-16"   "UA1-16r"  "UA1-3"   
## [187] "UA1-7"    "UA2-11"   "UA2-14"   "UA2-16"   "UA2-19"   "UA2-2"   
## [193] "UA2-5"    "UA2-7"

Population statistics

Selecting only population with at least six samples

Samples <- read_delim("Coordinates_pops_min5.txt", delim = "\t", escape_double = FALSE, trim_ws = TRUE)
## Rows: 254 Columns: 3
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr (1): SampleID
## dbl (2): Latitude, Longitude
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Fst and Nei’s D between populations

keep_ids <- intersect(indNames(dat2.pre_gl), Samples$SampleID)
pop(dat2.pre_gl) <- as.factor(sub("-.*", "",  dat2.pre_gl$ind.names)) # retract the regions (Pop)
gl_filtered <- dat2.pre_gl[indNames(dat2.pre_gl) %in% keep_ids, ]


indNames(gl_filtered) <- make.names(indNames(gl_filtered), unique = TRUE)
locNames(gl_filtered) <- make.names(locNames(gl_filtered), unique = TRUE)

stampp_data <- stamppConvert(gl_filtered, type = "genlight")

fst_matrix <- stamppFst(stampp_data, nboots = 1000, percent = 95, nclusters = 9)
Nei_matrix <- stamppNeisD(stampp_data, pop = TRUE)
fst_mat <- fst_matrix$Fsts
nei_mat <- Nei_matrix
colnames(nei_mat) <- rownames(nei_mat)

all_pops<-as.vector(unique(pop(gl_filtered)))
n <- length(all_pops)
fst_full <- matrix(NA, nrow=n, ncol=n, dimnames = list(all_pops, all_pops)) # to build full FST matrix
fst_full[match(rownames(fst_mat), all_pops),match(colnames(fst_mat), all_pops)] <- fst_mat

fst_sym <- fst_full
pop_order <- rownames(fst_sym)
fst_sym[upper.tri(fst_sym)] <- t(fst_sym)[upper.tri(fst_sym)]

# FST lower
fst_tri <- fst_sym
fst_tri[upper.tri(fst_tri, diag = TRUE)] <- NA
fst_df <- melt(fst_tri, na.rm = TRUE)

# Nei upper
nei_tri <- nei_mat
nei_tri[lower.tri(nei_tri, diag = TRUE)] <- NA
nei_df <- melt(nei_tri, na.rm = TRUE)

fst_df$Var1 <- factor(fst_df$Var1, levels = all_pops)
fst_df$Var2 <- factor(fst_df$Var2, levels = all_pops)
nei_df$Var1 <- factor(nei_df$Var1, levels = all_pops)
nei_df$Var2 <- factor(nei_df$Var2, levels = all_pops)

dim(fst_sym)
## [1] 25 25
dim(nei_mat)
## [1] 25 25
all(is.na(fst_tri[upper.tri(fst_tri)]))
## [1] TRUE
all(is.na(nei_tri[lower.tri(nei_tri)]))
## [1] TRUE
ggplot() + 
  geom_tile(data = fst_df,aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value),color = "white",linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_text(data = fst_df,aes(Var1, Var2, label = sprintf("%.2f", value)),size = 2,color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white",high = "#EE7733",name = expression(F[ST]) ) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
  geom_tile(data = nei_df,aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value),color = "white",linewidth = 0.2 ) +
  geom_text(data = nei_df,
    aes(Var1, Var2, label = sprintf("%.2f", value*100)),size = 2,color = "black" ) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white",high = "#009E73",name = "Nei’s D") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    aspect.ratio = 1,
    legend.position = "right" )

Ho, He and FIS per population

HoHeFis<-gl.report.heterozygosity(gl_filtered,  method = "pop", plot.colors.pop= "#009E73")
## Starting gl.report.heterozygosity 
##   Processing genlight object with SNP data
##   Calculating Observed Heterozygosities, averaged across
##                     loci, for each population
##   Calculating Expected Heterozygosities

## Completed: gl.report.heterozygosity

Total Ho, He and Fis

gl_filtered_no_pops <- dat2.pre_gl[indNames(dat2.pre_gl) %in% keep_ids, ]
pop(gl_filtered_no_pops) <- factor(rep("A", nInd(gl_filtered_no_pops))) # to assign all samples to one population
gl.report.heterozygosity(gl_filtered_no_pops,  method = "pop", plot.colors.pop= "#009E73")
